BOTH PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES TO DEPARTMENT 501 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 7, 2025. MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar May-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT CONSUMER DEFENSE LAW GROUP, P.C. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CONSUMER LEGAL DEFENSE GROUP PC), ANTHONY CARA, FERNANDO LEONE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is DENIED. Defendants failed to meet their burden of proving by a preponderance of the evidence that the electronic signature on the Retainer Agreement is authentic. (Civ. Code, section1633.9, subd. (a); Evid. Code, section 1401; see also, Ruiz v. Moss Bros. Auto Group, Inc. (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 836, 843-845; Fabian v. Renovate America, Inc. (2019) 42 Cal.App.5th 1062, 1068-1069.)

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
